info.title: Legislation Public API
info.description: The Federal Register of Legislation (the Legislation Register) is the authorised whole-of-government website for Commonwealth legislation and related documents. It contains the full text and details of the lifecycle of individual laws and the relationships between them. The Legislation Register is managed by the Office of Parliamentary Counsel in accordance with the Legislation Act 2003.

get.summary: Returns a content document.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content document.

200.description: Success - the requested content document is downloaded.

404.description: Not Found - the content document could not be found.

get.summary: Returns a list of departments. The list can be queried with standard ODATA commands.

200.description: Success - a list of departments

get.summary: Returns a list of documents. The list can be queried with standard ODATA commands.

200.description: Success - a list of documents.

get.summary:
Returns a document based on the unique identifier specified. By default it will return the document as bytes.
If you would like the document metadata, you need add an "Accept" header of "application/json".

parameters.name: keytitleId
parameters.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

parameters.name: keystart
parameters.description: The exact start date of the document version (yyyy-mm-dd).

parameters.name: keyretrospectiveStart
parameters.description: The exact retrospective start date of the document version (yyyy-mm-dd).

parameters.name: keyrectificationVersionNumber
parameters.description: The version number for the rectification. Will be 0 if this is not a rectification.

parameters.name: keytype
parameters.description: The type of document.

schema.description:
The type of document associated with legislation.

Members:

  • Primary - Acts of Parliament are sometimes referred to as primary legislation. Acts can authorise a person or body other than the Parliament to make laws on matters of detail, that is, secondary legislation.
  • ES - An explanatory statement or ES is a document that sets out how a legislative instrument is expected to operate and details about individual provisions
  • SupportingMaterial - Any other documents supporting the legislation.
  • IncorporatedByReference - Incorporated by reference is the act of including a second document within another document by only mentioning the second document, making the entire second document a part of the main document.
  • SupplementaryES - A statement to amend an initial or replacement explanatory statement and approved by the rule-maker.

parameters.name: keyuniqueTypeNumber
parameters.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

parameters.name: keyvolumeNumber
parameters.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

parameters.name: keyformat
parameters.description: The document format.

schema.description:
The file type of the legislation document.

Members:

  • Word - Microsoft Word document (.docx) file.
  • Pdf - Adobe Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.
  • Epub - Electronic publication open eBook (.epub) file.
  • NameOnly - Name only

200.description: Success - the requested document is downloaded or metadata returned.

404.description: Not Found - a document with the key supplied could not be found.

get.summary: Returns a file from within an EPUB file based on the unique identifier and path specified.

parameters.name: keytitleId
parameters.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

parameters.name: keystart
parameters.description: The exact start date of the document version (yyyy-mm-dd).

parameters.name: keyretrospectiveStart

parameters.name: keyrectificationVersionNumber
parameters.description: The version number for the rectification. Will be 0 if this is not a rectification.

parameters.name: keytype
parameters.description: The type of document.

schema.description:

Members:

  • Primary - Acts of Parliament are sometimes referred to as primary legislation. Acts can authorise a person or body other than the Parliament to make laws on matters of detail, that is, secondary legislation.
  • ES - An explanatory statement or ES is a document that sets out how a legislative instrument is expected to operate and details about individual provisions
  • SupportingMaterial - Any other documents supporting the legislation.
  • IncorporatedByReference - Incorporated by reference is the act of including a second document within another document by only mentioning the second document, making the entire second document a part of the main document.
  • SupplementaryES - A statement to amend an initial or replacement explanatory statement and approved by the rule-maker.

parameters.name: keyuniqueTypeNumber
parameters.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

parameters.name: keyvolumeNumber
parameters.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

parameters.name: keyformat
parameters.description: The document format.

schema.description:
The file type of the legislation document.

Members:

  • Word - Microsoft Word document (.docx) file.
  • Pdf - Adobe Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.
  • Epub - Electronic publication open eBook (.epub) file.
  • NameOnly - Name only

parameters.name: residualPath
parameters.description: The path to file within the EPUB.

200.description: Success - the requested file is downloaded.

404.description: Not Found - the requested file could not be found.

get.summary: Returns the list of files from within an EPUB file based on the unique identifier specified.

parameters.name: keystart

parameters.name: keyuniqueTypeNumber
parameters.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

parameters.name: keyvolumeNumber
parameters.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

parameters.name: keyformat
parameters.description: The document format.

schema.description:
The file type of the legislation document.

Members:

  • Word - Microsoft Word document (.docx) file.
  • Pdf - Adobe Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.
  • Epub - Electronic publication open eBook (.epub) file.
  • NameOnly - Name only

200.description: Success - a list of files within the EPUB are returned.

404.description: Not Found - an EPUB with the key supplied could not be found.

get.summary:
Returns a document based on the criteria specified. By default it will return the document as bytes.
If you would like the document metadata, you need add an "Accept" header of "application/json".

parameters.name: titleId
parameters.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

parameters.name: asAt
parameters.description: The as at date for the version (yyyy-mm-dd).

parameters.name: rectificationVersionNumber
parameters.description: The version number for the rectification. Will be 0 if this is not a rectification.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The document type.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The document format.

parameters.name: uniqueTypeNumber
parameters.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

parameters.name: volumeNumber
parameters.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

200.description: Success - the document matching the criteria is downloaded or metadata returned.

404.description: Not Found - a document matching the criteria could not be found.

post.description:
Sample request:
            
    POST /v1/publicfeedback
    {
        "subject":"",
        "message":"",
        "senderName":"John Citizen",
        "senderEmail":"john@somewhere.com",
        "refererUrl":"https://www.legislation.gov.au",
        "clientDetails": {
            "operatingSystem":"OSX11",
            "browser":"Chrome101",
            "isMobile":false,
            "areCookiesEnabled":true,
            "screenSize":"1920px x 937px",
            "windowSize":"1920px x 1040px",
            "userAgent":"Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.64 Safari/537.36"
        }
    }

200.description: Success - the feedback has been saved.

400.description: Bad Request - the feedback is invalid.

post.summary:
Generates a new session and returns an authorisation token for accessing secured endpoints.
On successful creation an auth_token value is returned. This value needs to be passed in the request header
when authentication is required. e.g. Authorization: Bearer {auth_token}.

requestBody.description: User name and password of a system user

200.description: Success - the login was successfully and token generated.

    }

schema.description:
A predefined point in time of a version.

Members:

  • Latest - The latest registered version that is in force.
  • AsMade - As made legislation is legislation in the form in which it was originally made. The text may not reflect in force as it will not include any later amendments that may have been made.
  • Current - The current version which may not yet be inforce.

200.description: Success - the version matching the criteria is returned.

404.description: Not Found - a version matching the criteria could not be found.

affectingTitleId.description: The identifier of the Affecting title (e.g. C2004A01224).

affectingProvisions.description: The affecting provisions in the affecting title.

affectedTitleId.description: The identifier of the Affected title (e.g. C2004A01224).

affectedProvisions.description: The affectedprovisions in the affected title.

Affect.description: An affect describes the function of the title.

titleId.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224) that is affected by.

name.description: The name of the title being affected by.

provisions.description: The provision of the title being affected by.

AffectedByTitle.description: Details of the affected by title.

ClientDetails.description: Details about the device used by a user of the system.

DateChanged.description: A structure to hold date change details.

id.description: Australian Government Organisations Register (AGOR) Id of the Department

portfolio.description: What portfolio the department falls under

Department.description: A reference within the law to a Department means the Australian Government department whose Minister administers the matter to which the reference relates.

titleId.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

start.description: The start date of the version of the title

retrospectiveStart.description: The retrospective start date of the title version (if different to the start date).

compilationNumber.description: The compilation number if the legislation is a compilation. Otherwise, it will be '0'.

registerId.description: The register id of the item (e.g. C2004A01224).

rectificationVersionNumber.description: The version number for the rectification. Will be 0 if this is not a rectification.

type.description:
The document type.

Members:

  • Primary - Acts of Parliament are sometimes referred to as primary legislation. Acts can authorise a person or body other than the Parliament to make laws on matters of detail, that is, secondary legislation.
  • ES - An explanatory statement or ES is a document that sets out how a legislative instrument is expected to operate and details about individual provisions
  • SupportingMaterial - Any other documents supporting the legislation.
  • IncorporatedByReference - Incorporated by reference is the act of including a second document within another document by only mentioning the second document, making the entire second document a part of the main document.
  • SupplementaryES - A statement to amend an initial or replacement explanatory statement and approved by the rule-maker.

uniqueTypeNumber.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

volumeNumber.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

format.description:
The file format of the document.

Members:

  • Word - Microsoft Word document (.docx) file.
  • Pdf - Adobe Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.
  • Epub - Electronic publication open eBook (.epub) file.
  • NameOnly - Name only

extension.description: The file extension of the document.

mimeType.description: The mime-type used for file download.

fileName.description: The file name of the source document.

pageCount.description: The number of pages in the document. Only used for PDF files.

sizeInBytes.description: The size of the file in bytes.

rectificationReason.description: The reason for the rectification if its a rectification.

isAuthorised.description: Whether the file has an authorised footer stamped. Only used for PDF files.

name.description: The custom name added to the document.

contents.description:
A user entered description of the contents of a particular volume. 
This is only non-null for documents with more than one volume

Document.description: Documents are associated with a Title and are the authorised copies and supporting information for a legislative title.

titleId.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

start.description: The start date of the version of the title

retrospectiveStart.description: The retrospective start date of the title version (if different to the start date)

rectificationVersionNumber.description: The version number for the rectification. Will be 0 if this is not a rectification.

type.description:
The document type.

Members:

  • Primary - Acts of Parliament are sometimes referred to as primary legislation. Acts can authorise a person or body other than the Parliament to make laws on matters of detail, that is, secondary legislation.
  • ES - An explanatory statement or ES is a document that sets out how a legislative instrument is expected to operate and details about individual provisions
  • SupportingMaterial - Any other documents supporting the legislation.
  • IncorporatedByReference - Incorporated by reference is the act of including a second document within another document by only mentioning the second document, making the entire second document a part of the main document.
  • SupplementaryES - A statement to amend an initial or replacement explanatory statement and approved by the rule-maker.

uniqueTypeNumber.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

volumeNumber.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

format.description:
The file format of the document.

Members:

  • Word - Microsoft Word document (.docx) file.
  • Pdf - Adobe Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.
  • Epub - Electronic publication open eBook (.epub) file.
  • NameOnly - Name only

titleId.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

start.description: The start date of the version of the title

retrospectiveStart.description: The retrospective start date of the title version (if different to the start date).

rectificationVersionNumber.description: The version number for the rectification. Will be 0 if this is not a rectification.

type.description:
The document type.

Members:

  • Primary - Acts of Parliament are sometimes referred to as primary legislation. Acts can authorise a person or body other than the Parliament to make laws on matters of detail, that is, secondary legislation.
  • ES - An explanatory statement or ES is a document that sets out how a legislative instrument is expected to operate and details about individual provisions
  • SupportingMaterial - Any other documents supporting the legislation.
  • IncorporatedByReference - Incorporated by reference is the act of including a second document within another document by only mentioning the second document, making the entire second document a part of the main document.
  • SupplementaryES - A statement to amend an initial or replacement explanatory statement and approved by the rule-maker.

uniqueTypeNumber.description: A unique identifier of a document when multiple documents of the same document type exist for a version of a title.

volumeNumber.description: The volume number for a multi-volume document type. Will be 0 if this is the only volume.

format.description:
The file format of the document.

Members:

  • Word - Microsoft Word document (.docx) file.
  • Pdf - Adobe Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.
  • Epub - Electronic publication open eBook (.epub) file.
  • NameOnly - Name only

pricePerCopy.description: The price of a printed copy of the document (only valid for PDFs).

DocumentPrint.description: Identifying details of a document, along with the price of ordering a copy. Only applicable to PDF document format types.

Frl.Api.Public.V1.Dtos.PrintOrderLineIte.description: A generic construct to provide custom print order lines.

NameHistory.description: A history of legislation name changes including the date of the change.

id.description: A unique identifier for the event.

eventType.description:
The type of parliamentary scrutiny event.

Members:

  • Tabled - When documents are presented to the Australian Parliament for scrutiny, they are said to have been tabled. This term dates back to historic practices, when documents were actually placed on a table in each House of the Parliament. All legislative instruments are required to be tabled shortly after registration.
  • DisallowanceMotion - Disallowance is a form of repeal of disallowable instruments initiated by the Parliament. The Parliament, with a majority vote in either House of the Parliament, may disallow a disallowable instrument in part or in full. This may result in an instrument ceasing to have effect and reviving an earlier instrument. If an instrument is disallowed, generally the rule-maker may not make an instrument similar in substance for 6 months.
  • MotionOutcome - An outcome of the parliamentary scrutiny motion.

house.description:
The two Houses of Parliament (the House of Representatives and the Senate)

Members:

  • HouseOfReps
  • Senate

isProrogueReTable.description: Whether the table event is because parliament was prorogued.

disallowanceMotionProvisionsForPartial.description: The provisions for disallowance motion if raised against the tabled item.

disallowanceMotionSponsor.description: Who sponsored the disallowance motion if raised against the tabled item.

disallowanceMotionExpiryDate.description: The expiry date for resolving the disallowance motion.

disallowanceMotionOutcomeId.description: The identifier of a disallowance motion if a disallowance motion is raised against the tabled item.

ParliamentaryScrutinyEvent.description: The details of a parliamentary scrutiny event.

PrintOrder.description: The print order structured for the printing providers.

numberOfCopiesOfEach.description: The number of print copies requested.

bindingOption.description:
The binding option for the print request.

Members:

  • Standard
  • Drilling
  • Wire

items.description:
Extra options about for the print order.

Members:

  • CoverArtwork - Include cover artwork.
  • PmsPrinting - Use Pantone Matching System (PMS) colour matching system for the printing.
  • CmykPrinting - Use Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Key (Black) system for the printing.

printOptions.description: Print options of the print request.

documents.description: The documents to be included in th print request.

PublicFeedback.description: Details about user feedback for a user of legislation.gov.au.

affect.description:
The reason for the affect.

Members:

  • AsMade - The as made version.
  • Amend - An amendment version.
  • Repeal - A repeal version.
  • Cease - A ceasing version.
  • ChangeDate - A change of date version.

markdown.description: The HTML Markdown summarising the reason for the version.

ReasonForVersion.description: A reason for a version being created.

category.description:
The category of a user saved search

Members:

  • SavedSearch - A saved collection of search criteria saved to the users My Account profile
  • SavedTitle - A saved search criteria representing a single title
  • Subscription - A saved collection of search criteria, which the user will be notified by email of if any of the titles change

SavedSearch.description: Details about a saved search associated with a user.

authToken.description: A token to be used as a bearer token when authenticated requests are required.

expiresIn.description: The number of seconds the token is valid for.

createdDateTime.description: The date and time of the session was started.

userName.description: The user the session was created for.

Session.description: Details about a user session on the system.

status.description:
The status at the point in time.

Members:

  • InForce - In force legislation is legislation that is on the statute book and has the force of law. On the Legislation Register, legislation that has been made but not yet commenced is also displayed in the in force listings. In force law does not include draft legislation such as Bills, repealed legislation or legislation that may not have been formally repealed but has otherwise ceased to be in force
  • Ceased - Legislation that may not have been formally repealed but has otherwise ceased to be in force.
  • Repealed - A repeal is the removal of a law or provision of that law from the statute book. If a provision is repealed, a new compilation will be prepared to remove the provision. A law that has been repealed will display on the Legislation Register as no longer in force.
  • NeverEffective - Legislation that never became in force.

reasons.description: The reasons for the version of the status.

StatusHistory.description: A history of legislation status changes including the date and reason of the change.

id.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

makingDate.description: The date the title was signed, sealed and otherwise endorsed.

isPrincipal.description: Principal legislation, as opposed to amending legislation, is the legislation that deals with a particular topic or area of the law. Whilst principal legislation may amend other legislation, its primary job is to set out new law rather than amend other laws.

isInForce.description: Whether the title is currently in force.

publishComments.description: Comments when the title was published.

status.description:
The status of title.

Members:

  • InForce - In force legislation is legislation that is on the statute book and has the force of law. On the Legislation Register, legislation that has been made but not yet commenced is also displayed in the in force listings. In force law does not include draft legislation such as Bills, repealed legislation or legislation that may not have been formally repealed but has otherwise ceased to be in force
  • Ceased - Legislation that may not have been formally repealed but has otherwise ceased to be in force.
  • Repealed - A repeal is the removal of a law or provision of that law from the statute book. If a provision is repealed, a new compilation will be prepared to remove the provision. A law that has been repealed will display on the Legislation Register as no longer in force.
  • NeverEffective - Legislation that never became in force.

hasCommencedUnincorporatedAmendments.description: Whether there are amendments for the title that have not been incorporated and are currently in force

nameHistory.description: A history of legislation name changes including the date of the change from the past.

namePossibleFuture.description: A history of legislation name changes including the date of the change in the future.

statusHistory.description: A history of legislation status changes including the date and reason of the change from the past.

statusPossibleFuture.description: A history of legislation status changes including the date and reason of the change in the future.

authorisedBy.description: Affects that authorised the title. To view this value you will require query string parameter: $expand=AuthorisedBy

administeringDepartments.description: The departments that are administrating the title. To view this value you will require query string parameter: $expand=AdministeringDepartments

parliamentaryScrutiny.description: Details of the legislation being scrutinized in either House of Parliament. To view this value you will require query string parameter: $expand=ParliamentaryScrutiny

originatingBillUri.description: The uri of a page which has details about the Bill that this Act was created from.  If this title is NOT an Act, this will always be null.

asMadeRegisteredAt.description: The date the As Made version was registered on the FRL.

Title.description: The title of a law refers to a law's official title or name.

homeSite.description:
The legislation.gov.au portal type.

Members:

  • Lodgement - The lodgement site used by Government agencies to support their legislation.
  • Public - The public site that allows anonymous users to search, view and download legislation.
  • Management - The management site used by the Office of Parliamentary Counsel staff to manage legislation.

titleId.description: The identifier of the title (e.g. C2004A01224).

start.description: The start date of the version of the title.

retrospectiveStart.description: The retrospective start date of the title version (if different to the start date).

end.description: The end date of the version of the title.

retrospectiveEnd.description: The retrospective end date of the title version (if different to the end date).

isCurrent.description: Whether this is the current version of the title (may not be registered).

isLatest.description: Whether this is the latest registered version of the title.

name.description: The name of the title at the time of this version.

status.description:
The status of this version of the title.

Members:

  • InForce - In force legislation is legislation that is on the statute book and has the force of law. On the Legislation Register, legislation that has been made but not yet commenced is also displayed in the in force listings. In force law does not include draft legislation such as Bills, repealed legislation or legislation that may not have been formally repealed but has otherwise ceased to be in force
  • Ceased - Legislation that may not have been formally repealed but has otherwise ceased to be in force.
  • Repealed - A repeal is the removal of a law or provision of that law from the statute book. If a provision is repealed, a new compilation will be prepared to remove the provision. A law that has been repealed will display on the Legislation Register as no longer in force.
  • NeverEffective - Legislation that never became in force.

registerId.description: If registered, the register id of the item.

registeredAt.description: If registered, the date the version title is registered.

compilationNumber.description: The compilation number if the legislation is a compilation. Otherwise, it will be '0'.

publishComments.description: Comments when the version was published.

hasUnincorporatedAmendments.description: Whether there are amendments for the title that have not been incorporated.

reasons.description: The reasons for the version of title.

documents.description: Documents associated with the version of title.

Version.description: A version is a view of a Title at a particular point in time. As amendments are made to the law new versions of the law are compiled and published.
